Idea to Product
Our Story

Why we built vigOR.

We are a team of Computer Science students participating in our first hackathon, driven by a shared goal to build meaningful technology that solves real-world problems.

Through this project, we aim not only to create a practical solution for asthma safety, but also to strengthen our skills in coding, problem solving, and system design.

More importantly, this experience allows us to grow as a team — improving our communication, collaboration, and ability to turn ideas into working products.

vigOR represents both our technical learning journey and our commitment to using technology to make everyday life safer.

Code & Craft

We built vigOR from scratch — every line written, debugged, and deployed by our team during the hackathon.

Teamwork First

Communication and collaboration were our foundation. We divided by strength and united by purpose.

Problem-Driven

We didn't build for the demo. Oregon's asthma crisis is real — we built vigOR because the numbers are unacceptable.

Always Learning

First hackathon means first time for everything. We embraced every obstacle as a lesson and every bug as a teacher.

// Hackathon · 2025

Our first build.
Our real mission.

From whiteboard sketches to a working platform in one weekend — vigOR was born at a hackathon but built for the long run. We brought together air quality APIs, medical record encryption, AI health chat, and live route analysis under one roof.

Python Flask Google Maps API Anthropic AI AES-256 AQI Live Data Firebase Auth
Join Us

Built by students.
For everyone who breathes.

vigOR is free during public beta. No credit card. No friction. Just better air awareness.
